channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: NSW and Victoria conduct nation-first early learning compliance blitz
item.description: The NSW Early Learning Commission and the Victorian Early Childhood Regulatory Authority (VECRA) have joined forces in a nation-leading compliance operation focused on child safety across the early childhood education and care sector.
item.content:encoded: The NSW Early Learning Commission and the Victorian Early Childhood Regulatory Authority (VECRA) have joined forces in a nation-leading compliance operation focused on child safety across the early childhood education and care sector.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Victorian Early Years Awards nominations open to celebrate excellence across the early years sector
item.description: Nominations for the 2026 Victorian Early Years Awards (VEYA) open Monday 4 May, recognising the professionals, programs and organisations making a meaningful difference for children and families across Victoria.
item.content:encoded: Nominations for the 2026 Victorian Early Years Awards (VEYA) open Monday 4 May, recognising the professionals, programs and organisations making a meaningful difference for children and families across Victoria.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Sesame Workshop Expands Emotional Well-Being Resources for Young Children
item.description: The nonprofit organisation releases new content to help families talk about big feelings and partners with publishers on books to expand on wellbeing topics
item.content:encoded: The nonprofit organisation releases new content to help families talk about big feelings and partners with publishers on books to expand on wellbeing topics
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: OECD releases landmark international study on early learning and development
item.description: Understanding how children learn and develop in the early years is central to supporting strong educational outcomes and lifelong wellbeing.
item.content:encoded: Understanding how children learn and develop in the early years is central to supporting strong educational outcomes and lifelong wellbeing.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: UK early years focus offers lessons for Australia as Princess of Wales-backed guide launches
item.description: The Royal Foundation Centre for Early Childhood has launched Foundations for Life, a new guide focused on social and emotional development in the earliest years. For Australia’s early childhood education and care sector, the UK initiative offers a timely reminder that access reform must be matched by investment in relationships, workforce capability and family support.
item.content:encoded: The Royal Foundation Centre for Early Childhood has launched Foundations for Life, a new guide focused on social and emotional development in the earliest years. For Australia’s early childhood education and care sector, the UK initiative offers a timely reminder that access reform must be matched by investment in relationships, workforce capability and family support.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: The earlier kids are in childcare the better, minister declares - The Australian
item.description: The earlier kids are in childcare the better, minister declares - The Australian
item.content:encoded: The earlier kids are in childcare the better, minister declares - The Australian
item.dc:creator: The Australian

item.title: Paid parental leave changes: what families and ECEC employers need to know
item.description: Australia’s Paid Parental Leave scheme is expanding, with more paid days, superannuation on government-funded payments and greater flexibility for families. For early childhood education and care employers, the changes may also affect workforce planning, rostering, staff retention, enrolment timing and centre profitability.
item.content:encoded: Australia’s Paid Parental Leave scheme is expanding, with more paid days, superannuation on government-funded payments and greater flexibility for families. For early childhood education and care employers, the changes may also affect workforce planning, rostering, staff retention, enrolment timing and centre profitability.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 8 May 2026
item.description: This week’s SNAICC in the News remembers Kumanjayi Little Baby and reflects on Sorry Business, community healing and Catherine Liddle’s calls for care.

item.title: Investigation needed into child protection leaks
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children is calling for an immediate investigation into the leaking of confidential information relating to child protection notifications involving Kumanjayi Little Baby and her family
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children is calling for an immediate investigation into the leaking of confidential information relating to child protection notifications involving Kumanjayi Little Baby and her family
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 1 May 2026
item.description: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.content:encoded: This is not the news we wanted to hear. This week has been a tragedy that has shattered the nation and devastated the community of Mparntwe/Alice Springs.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: National Child and Family Investment Strategy sets clear direction, now governments must deliver
item.description: SNAICC has welcomed the release of the National Child and Family Investment Strategy as a landmark framework to shift investment to ACCOs.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC has welcomed the release of the National Child and Family Investment Strategy as a landmark framework to shift investment to ACCOs.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.description: Op-Ed on supporting Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children means supporting the community-controlled sector

item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 17 April 2026
item.description: Sector Transformation Principles Framework continued to draw national coverage, Australia’s under-16 social media restrictions turned to the risks for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander young people in regional and remote communities, and after the WA Government announced a $45 million child protection package, we reiterate our calls to implement the long-promised WA Roadmap, and more.
item.content:encoded: Sector Transformation Principles Framework continued to draw national coverage, Australia’s under-16 social media restrictions turned to the risks for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander young people in regional and remote communities, and after the WA Government announced a $45 million child protection package, we reiterate our calls to implement the long-promised WA Roadmap, and more.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC
